name: Blueberry Buttermilk Pancakes
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Blueberries; washed, dried and rolled in flour
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, blend together the flour, salt, and baking soda.
2. In a separate bowl, whisk together the buttermilk, eggs, and melted butter until well combined.
3. Gradually stir the dry mixture into the wet mixture, just until combined. Be careful not to overbeat.
4. Gently fold in the blueberries.
5. Heat about 2 tablespoons of butter on a griddle or large skillet over medium heat.
6. Using a ladle or spoon, drop batter onto the hot griddle, forming pancakes that are about 3-4 tablespoons in size.
7. Cook until bubbles form on the surface and the edges start to look dry, then flip each pancake with a spatula.
8. Cook for an additional minute or so on the other side until golden brown.
9. Remove the pancakes from the skillet and keep warm.
This recipe was featured by Jane & Michael Stern in their column (A Taste of America) this week at Al's Breakfast in Minneapolis, where they commented, "It's those pancakes that won our hearts." Enjoy your meal!
